title: TECNALIA Strengthens Hydrogen Laboratory with H2TESTLAB, a Unique Materials Testing Infrastructure
content: TECNALIA has strengthened its hydrogen laboratory with H2TESTLAB, a unique infrastructure for testing materials and components aimed at the generation, distribution and use of renewable hydrogen, consolidating the laboratory as a benchmark for development, testing and demonstration of these technologies.  This extension is part of TECNALIAâ€™s goal to accelerate innovation and improve industrial competitiveness in the hydrogen value chain by constantly including new experimental capabilities and advanced infrastructure to offer a better service to companies and the wider industrial ecosystem.  With a focus on the development and scaling up of next generation electrolysers, the compatibility and durability of materials, storage, safety and industrial uses of hydrogen, H2TESTLAB joins the rest of the testing, validation and technological demonstration capabilities across TECNALIAâ€™s sites.  The project has enabled the creation of unique renewable hydrogen infrastructure and equipment in Donostia San Sebastian, including an AEM electrolyser test bench to assess advanced renewable hydrogen production solutions, an electrolysis membranes test bench focused on performance and material degradation, an embrittlement and material compatibility test bench to assess safety, reliability and durability, a hydrogen sensor test bench to validate detection and monitoring, and general laboratory infrastructure that integrates control electronics, safety and hydrogen specific ventilation systems.  TECNALIA has led the H2TESTLAB project through Programme 1 of the PERTE ERHA call for aid by IDAE to promote capacities, technological advances and test or manufacturing lines in the renewable hydrogen industrial value chain, within the Recovery, Transformation and Resilience Plan.
author: Analysts Account
